text: When you write code, you will inevitably encounter bugs, or errors that stop your program from working correctly. The first step is identifying bugs by carefully reading error messages. A systematic approach is crucial: instead of guessing, test one part of your code at a time. You can use debugging tools like breakpoints in your editor, which pause the program so you can inspect variable values. Another helpful strategy is logging, where you print messages to track what your code is doing. By staying patient and methodical, you can find and fix issues more quickly.
